CheckBeforeCreateInstance.zone: Availability zone, e.g. ZoneA, ZoneB, etc.
CheckBeforeCreateInstance.vpcId: VPC (short ID) where the RapidFS instance resides
CheckBeforeCreateInstance.subnetId: Subnet (short ID) where the RapidFS instance resides
CheckBeforeCreateInstance.managedMode:
Deployment mode for RapidFS instance. Enum values:
• FullyManaged: Fully managed deployment mode, suitable for businesses without idle storage resources, at ultra-large scale, or requiring high stability. In this mode, RapidFS fully manages the instance with no user maintenance required;
• MasterManaged: Master-managed deployment mode, suitable for businesses with idle storage resources, cost-sensitive requirements, and certain operational capabilities.
CheckBeforeCreateInstance.metaSpec:
Metadata specification, currently effective only in MasterManaged deployment mode. Enum values:
• M1: Applicable to scenarios with up to 1 billion files, a maximum of 50 cache nodes, and up to 4 data disks mountable per cache node;
• M2: Applicable to scenarios with up to 5 billion files, a maximum of 400 cache nodes, and up to 8 data disks mountable per cache node.
CheckBeforeCreateInstance.dataSpec:
Data specification, currently effective only in FullyManaged deployment mode. Enum values:
• Basic: Data performance baseline of 100 MB/s/TiB;
• Capacity: Data performance baseline of 200 MB/s/TiB.
CheckBeforeCreateInstance.type:
Cache access type managed by the user under MasterManaged deployment mode. Valid only in MasterManaged mode. Enum values:
• BCC: Cloud server BCC, indicating cache nodes are deployed directly on BCC cloud servers;
• IDC: Self-built data center, indicating cache nodes are deployed directly on user IDC servers;
• CCE: Cloud Container Engine, indicating cache nodes are deployed as containers in a CCE cluster;
• K8S: Self-managed Kubernetes, including in-house EKS or self-built K8s, indicating cache nodes are deployed as containers in an in-house EKS or self-built K8s cluster;
• AIHC: AI Heterogeneous Compute (Baidu AIHC), indicating cache nodes are deployed as containers in an AIHC cluster; currently only fully managed AIHC resource pools are supported.
CheckBeforeCreateInstance.capacityTiB: Required in FullyManaged mode. Specifies the purchased cache capacity in TiB. Must not exceed the maximum purchasable capacity returned by the DescribeSpecs API.
CheckBeforeCreateInstance.cceClusterId: Required in MasterManaged mode when the cache access type is CCE. Specifies the CCE cluster ID.
CheckBeforeCreateInstance.aihcResourcePoolId: Required in MasterManaged mode when the cache access type is AIHC. Specifies the AIHC fully managed resource pool ID.
CheckBeforeCreateInstance.k8sControllerId: Required in MasterManaged mode when the cache access type is K8S. Specifies the RapidFS cache controller ID within the K8s cluster. Retrieve it after controller installation via: `kubectl get cm -n kube-system rapidfs-cache-service-config -o yaml`
CheckBeforeCreateInstance.k8sControllerToken: Required in MasterManaged deployment mode when the user-managed cache access type is K8S; specifies the RapidFS cache controller Token inside the K8s cluster. After installing the controller, retrieve it via: `kubectl get cm -n kube-system rapidfs-cache-service-config -o yaml`. This field must be encrypted using the secretkey corresponding to request header x-bce-accesskey before transmission; see [Password Encryption Transmission Specification](https://cloud.baidu.com/doc/BCC/s/ojwvynrqn#%E5%AF%86%E7%A0%81%E5%8A%A0%E5%AF%86%E4%BC%A0%E8%BE%93%E8%A7%84%E8%8C%83). Refer to the [bce-python-sdk](https://cloud.baidu.com/doc/Developer/index.html) `aes128_encrypt_16char_key()` function for implementation.
